description: Middleware that turns a GraphQL schema into a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server: each Query/Mutation root field becomes an MCP tool described from the SDL, runnable side-by-side with your GraphQL server.
rules: The schema is the source of truth: a tool's name, description, and input schema mirror the GraphQL field one-to-one. Never hardcode domain type names in the library.

All public exports come from src/index.ts: createHttpHandler, createMcpServer, createServerFactory, registerGraphqlTools, buildTools, createLocalExecutor, createHttpExecutor, buildOperation, buildSelectionSet, argsToZodShape, plus types.

GraphqlExecutor is the single execution seam: a tool builds a { query, variables, operationName, context } request and hands it to the executor. The default is createLocalExecutor(schema) (in-process); createHttpExecutor forwards to a remote endpoint. Tools never inline argument values — they pass them as GraphQL variables.
Both Query and Mutation root fields become tools (MCP has no query/mutation split). Queries are annotated readOnlyHint/idempotentHint; mutations destructiveHint; all set openWorldHint.

Selection sets are auto-generated by buildSelectionSet: scalar/enum leaves at each level plus nested objects up to selectionDepth (default 2), always including __typename; fields requiring arguments and cyclic types are skipped.

buildTools produces pure ToolDescriptors (no SDK, no executor); server.ts binds them to an executor and an McpServer. Custom tools (the `tools` option) add to or override generated ones by name; registerTool throws on duplicate names so overrides are resolved before registering.

Stateless HTTP needs a fresh McpServer per request because a server owns a single transport; createHttpHandler builds descriptors once and mints a new server+transport per request. Express is assumed but not a dependency — the handler only needs a Node IncomingMessage with parsed req.body and a ServerResponse.

Stack: TypeScript 5.7+ strict, ESM only, erasable-syntax-only so source runs under Node's type stripping (.ts import specifiers, rewritten to .js by tsc). Tests use Node's built-in runner (node --test), no test framework. Biome for lint/format. Only runtime dependency is zod; @modelcontextprotocol/sdk and graphql are peer deps.
